# Transcrição: HOW_TO_INNOVATE_(THE_BEATLES_STRATEGY)

- **Fonte:** HOW_TO_INNOVATE_(THE_BEATLES_STRATEGY)
- **Duração:** 01:15
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 01:15]

This strategy was taken from The Beatles when they were writing the song Let It Be. When I try to create innovation in my business and I try to change things up, what I do is I don't rely on myself, I don't rely just on my team. I bring in different perspectives. For example, when I write a new keynote, I'll look for a comedian, I'll look for a magician, I'll look for a thought leader that I really like and I'll bring in two of my friends and I'll hire an Airbnb for a whole week and say that we're going to sit in this Airbnb, I'll pay all of you your time and what you're worth. All we're doing is we're bringing five worlds together to create something that hasn't been created before. The first two days we do nothing but play games, obviously, and then on day three we're like, hey, hey, hey, look, I think we need to get some work done. And then things start to happen on day three, day four, day five, day six, day seven, things start to get created. This strategy was taken from The Beatles when they were writing the song Let It Be. They locked themselves in the studio for 14 days plus and the song came around the last few days. So I create pressure, but I have pressure created with people who have completely different sets of experiences. Because innovation at the end of the day, what is it? Innovation is just two perspectives coming together that have never come together before. That's all. And in the moment of energy, passion, sleep deprivation, logic center of your brain, shut down already at that point so you can make unnatural connections that normally don't connect, then we're like, oh, that genius. But to me, that's where innovation comes from. Otherwise, if we keep the same minds and the same perspectives, nothing new happens.
